trade.summary: Substantial bilateral trade despite absence of formal diplomatic ties. Canada exports canola, copper, coal, and natural gas to Taiwan. Taiwan exports electronics, semiconductors, petrochemicals, and machinery to Canada. Taiwan is a critical supplier of semiconductors used in Canadian industries.

diplomatic.summary: Canada does not have formal diplomatic relations with Taiwan, recognizing the PRC. However, trade offices function effectively and Canada has been increasingly vocal in supporting Taiwan's international participation and opposing unilateral changes to the status quo. Canada-Taiwan relations have warmed as part of broader Western alignment on Taiwan.
recent_incidents: Canada joined G7 statements opposing coercion against Taiwan

military.summary: No formal military relations. Canada has joined allied statements about Taiwan Strait stability. Canadian naval vessels have transited the Taiwan Strait. Growing de facto security alignment.
incidents: Canadian naval transit of Taiwan Strait

financial.summary: Growing bilateral investment. Taiwanese semiconductor companies (TSMC supply chain) are investing in Canada. Canadian companies have Taiwan operations. Growing interest in critical minerals for semiconductor supply chains.

energy.summary: Canada exports LNG and coal to Taiwan. Taiwan is seeking to diversify energy sources. Canadian energy exports to Taiwan have been growing.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific cooperation in semiconductor technology, AI, and engineering. Taiwan-Canada university linkages are expanding. Joint research in materials science and quantum computing.
